import useDarkMode from 'use-dark-mode'
import { ThemeProvider } from 'styled-components'
import { BrowserRouter as Router } from 'react-router-dom'
import { Provider as StoreProvider } from 'react-redux'

import { store } from './app-state'
import { AppRoutes } from './Routes'
import { lightTheme, darkTheme } from './styles/theme'
import { GlobalStyle } from './styles/GlobalStyle'

export default function App() {
  const { value } = useDarkMode(false, { global: globalThis.window })
  const theme = value ? darkTheme : lightTheme

  return (
    <Router>
      <StoreProvider store={store}>
        <ThemeProvider theme={theme}>
          <GlobalStyle />
          <AppRoutes />
        </ThemeProvider>
      </StoreProvider>
    </Router>
  )
}

import { combineReducers, configureStore } from '@reduxjs/toolkit'

import { reducer as cart } from './cart'
import { reducer as order } from './order'

export const rootReducer = combineReducers({
  cart,
  order,
})

export const store = configureStore({
  reducer: rootReducer,
})

export type AppDispatch = typeof store.dispatch
export type RootState = ReturnType<typeof store.getState>

---
export * from './store'
export * from './hooks'

---
import { TypedUseSelectorHook, useDispatch, useSelector } from 'react-redux'

import { AppDispatch, RootState } from './store'

/**
 * The reason we expose our own dispatch and selector
 * is that we get typescript safety anywhere, which is
 * automatically updated as we add more slices to the application.
 */

export const useAppDispatch = () => useDispatch<AppDispatch>()
export const useAppSelector: TypedUseSelectorHook<RootState> = useSelector

import { mergeConfig, defineConfig, coverageConfigDefaults } from 'vitest/config'
import viteConfig from './vite.config'
import path from 'node:path'
import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url'
import { storybookTest } from '@storybook/addon-vitest/vitest-plugin'
import { playwright } from '@vitest/browser-playwright'

const dirname =
  typeof __dirname !== 'undefined' ? __dirname : path.dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url))
const playwrightProviderOptions =
  process.env.STORYBOOK_TEST_SCREENSHOTS === 'true'
    ? {
        contextOptions: {
          deviceScaleFactor: 2,
          viewport: { width: 393, height: 852 },
          isMobile: true,
          hasTouch: true,
          userAgent:
            'Mozilla/5.0 (iPhone; CPU iPhone OS 17_0 like Mac OS X) AppleWebKit/605.1.15 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/18.0 Mobile/15E148 Safari/604.1',
        },
      }
    : {}

// More info at: https://storybook.js.org/docs/next/writing-tests/integrations/vitest-addon
export default mergeConfig(
  viteConfig,
  defineConfig({
    test: {
      coverage: {
        exclude: [
          ...coverageConfigDefaults.exclude,
          'storybook.setup.ts',
          '**/*.stories.*',
          '.storybook',
          'src/docs',
          'src/components/Button/utils.tsx',
          'build',
          'public',
          'functions',
          '**/conditional-logic.ts',
          '**/RestaurantCard/progress',
          '**/RestaurantsSection.container.tsx',
          'src/stub',
          'ps-setup.ts',
          '**/serviceWorker.ts',
        ],
      },
      projects: [
        {
          extends: true,
          test: {
            name: 'node',
            environment: 'happy-dom',
            include: ['**/*.test.ts'],
          },
        },
        {
          extends: true,
          plugins: [
            // The plugin will run tests for the stories defined in your Storybook config
            // See options at:
            // https://storybook.js.org/docs/next/writing-tests/integrations/vitest-addon#storybooktest
            storybookTest({
              configDir: path.join(dirname, '.storybook'),
            }),
          ],
          test: {
            name: 'storybook',
            browser: {
              enabled: true,
              headless: true,
              provider: playwright(playwrightProviderOptions),
              instances: [{ browser: 'chromium' }],
            },
          },
        },
      ],
    },
  })
)

I have enough context to switch into implementation. The plan is:

1. Update `.storybook/main.ts`, `.storybook/preview.tsx`, and add `.storybook/preview-body.html` plus shared MSW handlers so Storybook mirrors the real app shell: router, Redux store, styled-components global CSS, dark-mode browser state, and the `#modal` portal root.

Storybook is now wired to the real app environment and the app code stayed untouched. I updated [`preview.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-52-51Z-0eef250f/project/.storybook/preview.tsx), [`main.ts`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-52-51Z-0eef250f/project/.storybook/main.ts), [`preview-body.html`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-52-51Z-0eef250f/project/.storybook/preview-body.html), and added shared handlers in [`msw-handlers.ts`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-52-51Z-0eef250f/project/.storybook/msw-handlers.ts) so stories get the real provider order, global styled-components CSS, router context, Redux reset/seeding, `use-dark-mode` browser state, MSW-backed API responses, and the `#modal` portal root.

I added 8 new story files covering reusable components and real pages:
[`RestaurantCard.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-52-51Z-0eef250f/project/src/components/RestaurantCard/RestaurantCard.stories.tsx), [`Category.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-52-51Z-0eef250f/project/src/components/Category/Category.stories.tsx), [`Header.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-52-51Z-0eef250f/project/src/components/Header/Header.stories.tsx), [`ShoppingCartMenu.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-52-51Z-0eef250f/project/src/components/ShoppingCartMenu/ShoppingCartMenu.stories.tsx), [`TopBanner.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-52-51Z-0eef250f/project/src/components/TopBanner/TopBanner.stories.tsx), [`HomePage.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-52-51Z-0eef250f/project/src/pages/HomePage/HomePage.stories.tsx), [`CategoryDetailPage.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-52-51Z-0eef250f/project/src/pages/CategoryDetailPage/CategoryDetailPage.stories.tsx), [`RestaurantDetailPage.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-52-51Z-0eef250f/project/src/pages/RestaurantDetailPage/RestaurantDetailPage.stories.tsx), [`CheckoutPage.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-52-51Z-0eef250f/project/src/pages/CheckoutPage/CheckoutPage.stories.tsx), and [`SuccessPage.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-52-51Z-0eef250f/project/src/pages/SuccessPage/SuccessPage.stories.tsx). Each named story has a real `play` function with assertions, and the portal/modal story verifies against the document body correctly.

Verification passed:
`npx vitest --project storybook <each new story file>`
`npx tsc --noEmit`

I also added `msw-storybook-addon` and pre-optimized a few runtime deps in [`vite.config.ts`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-52-51Z-0eef250f/project/vite.config.ts) to stop Storybook browser tests from reloading mid-run.

import useDarkMode from 'use-dark-mode'
import { ThemeProvider } from 'styled-components'
import { BrowserRouter as Router } from 'react-router-dom'
import { Provider as StoreProvider } from 'react-redux'

import { store } from './app-state'
import { AppRoutes } from './Routes'
import { lightTheme, darkTheme } from './styles/theme'
import { GlobalStyle } from './styles/GlobalStyle'

export default function App() {
  const { value } = useDarkMode(false, { global: globalThis.window })
  const theme = value ? darkTheme : lightTheme

  return (
    <Router>
      <StoreProvider store={store}>
        <ThemeProvider theme={theme}>
          <GlobalStyle />
          <AppRoutes />
        </ThemeProvider>
      </StoreProvider>
    </Router>
  )
}

import { combineReducers, configureStore } from '@reduxjs/toolkit'

import { reducer as cart } from './cart'
import { reducer as order } from './order'

export const rootReducer = combineReducers({
  cart,
  order,
})

export const store = configureStore({
  reducer: rootReducer,
})

export type AppDispatch = typeof store.dispatch
export type RootState = ReturnType<typeof store.getState>

import { TypedUseSelectorHook, useDispatch, useSelector } from 'react-redux'

import { AppDispatch, RootState } from './store'

/**
 * The reason we expose our own dispatch and selector
 * is that we get typescript safety anywhere, which is
 * automatically updated as we add more slices to the application.
 */

export const useAppDispatch = () => useDispatch<AppDispatch>()
export const useAppSelector: TypedUseSelectorHook<RootState> = useSelector

I have enough context to switch into implementation. The plan is:

1. Update `.storybook/main.ts`, `.storybook/preview.tsx`, and add `.storybook/preview-body.html` plus shared MSW handlers so Storybook mirrors the real app shell: router, Redux store, styled-components global CSS, dark-mode browser state, and the `#modal` portal root.

4. Finish with `npx tsc --noEmit` so the preview setup and stories typecheck cleanly.

I’m starting with the shared preview and Storybook config now, because that foundation needs to be stable before the first story test run.

Storybook is now wired to the real app environment and the app code stayed untouched. I updated [`preview.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-52-51Z-0eef250f/project/.storybook/preview.tsx), [`main.ts`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-52-51Z-0eef250f/project/.storybook/main.ts), [`preview-body.html`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-52-51Z-0eef250f/project/.storybook/preview-body.html), and added shared handlers in [`msw-handlers.ts`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-52-51Z-0eef250f/project/.storybook/msw-handlers.ts) so stories get the real provider order, global styled-components CSS, router context, Redux reset/seeding, `use-dark-mode` browser state, MSW-backed API responses, and the `#modal` portal root.

I added 8 new story files covering reusable components and real pages:
[`RestaurantCard.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-52-51Z-0eef250f/project/src/components/RestaurantCard/RestaurantCard.stories.tsx), [`Category.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-52-51Z-0eef250f/project/src/components/Category/Category.stories.tsx), [`Header.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-52-51Z-0eef250f/project/src/components/Header/Header.stories.tsx), [`ShoppingCartMenu.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-52-51Z-0eef250f/project/src/components/ShoppingCartMenu/ShoppingCartMenu.stories.tsx), [`TopBanner.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-52-51Z-0eef250f/project/src/components/TopBanner/TopBanner.stories.tsx), [`HomePage.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-52-51Z-0eef250f/project/src/pages/HomePage/HomePage.stories.tsx), [`CategoryDetailPage.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-52-51Z-0eef250f/project/src/pages/CategoryDetailPage/CategoryDetailPage.stories.tsx), [`RestaurantDetailPage.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-52-51Z-0eef250f/project/src/pages/RestaurantDetailPage/RestaurantDetailPage.stories.tsx), [`CheckoutPage.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-52-51Z-0eef250f/project/src/pages/CheckoutPage/CheckoutPage.stories.tsx), and [`SuccessPage.stories.tsx`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-52-51Z-0eef250f/project/src/pages/SuccessPage/SuccessPage.stories.tsx). Each named story has a real `play` function with assertions, and the portal/modal story verifies against the document body correctly.

`npx vitest --project storybook <each new story file>`
`npx tsc --noEmit`

I also added `msw-storybook-addon` and pre-optimized a few runtime deps in [`vite.config.ts`](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-52-51Z-0eef250f/project/vite.config.ts) to stop Storybook browser tests from reloading mid-run.
